en.name: DIGESTIVE SYSTEM
en.info: Inspect the full digestive system in AR and tap the numbered labels to read the role of each main structure.
en.hint: Use rotate, scale and labels while the model is active.

parts.label: Liver
parts.title: LIVER
parts.info: The liver supports digestion by producing bile and helping the body process nutrients.
parts.hint: This large upper organ helps chemical digestion.

parts.label: Stomach
parts.title: STOMACH
parts.info: The stomach stores food for a short time, mixes it, and starts chemical digestion.
parts.hint: It works like a mixing chamber before food moves onward.

parts.label: Pancreas
parts.title: PANCREAS
parts.info: The pancreas helps digestion by releasing enzymes that continue breaking food down.
parts.hint: This structure works with the stomach and intestine during digestion.

parts.label: Small Intestine
parts.title: SMALL INTESTINE
parts.info: Most digestion finishes in the small intestine, where nutrients are absorbed into the body.
parts.hint: This is the main absorption zone of the digestive system.

parts.label: Large Intestine
parts.title: LARGE INTESTINE
parts.info: The large intestine reabsorbs water and helps prepare waste before it leaves the body.
parts.hint: It forms the outer lower frame around the intestinal area.

parts.label: Lower Digestive Tract
parts.title: LOWER DIGESTIVE TRACT
parts.info: This lower section completes the digestive pathway and helps the body eliminate waste.
parts.hint: It marks the final stage of the digestive process.
